project.name: RFID Wallet Transaction System
project.description: A production-grade RFID-based wallet system enabling top-up and payment operations with real-time dashboard updates.
project.vision: To provide a secure, real-time, and scalable RFID payment solution for campus or small business environments, demonstrating architectural discipline with clear separation of MQTT, HTTP, and WebSocket layers.

functional.description: Card Top-Up: Admin can increase a card's balance via the dashboard.
acceptance_criteria: Admin enters card UID and amount, clicks 'TOP UP'.
System validates amount > 0.
Backend publishes MQTT command to ESP8266.
ESP8266 updates local balance and publishes new balance.
Backend updates database atomically and broadcasts new balance via WebSocket.
Dashboard displays success and new balance.

functional.description: Card Payment: Cashier can deduct balance for a product purchase.
acceptance_criteria: Cashier selects product, enters quantity, and card UID.
System calculates total and checks balance.
If insufficient, returns error and no transaction recorded.
If sufficient, publishes MQTT payment command to ESP8266.
ESP8266 deducts balance, publishes new balance.
Backend atomically updates database and broadcasts new balance.
Dashboard shows approved/declined with reason.

functional.description: Real-time Balance Updates: Dashboard reflects balance changes instantly via WebSocket.
acceptance_criteria: WebSocket connection established on dashboard load.
Any balance update triggers a broadcast to all connected clients.
Dashboard UI updates without refresh.

functional.description: MQTT Topic Isolation: All communication uses team-specific topics.
acceptance_criteria: ESP8266 publishes to 'rfid/kawacukennedy/card/status' and 'rfid/kawacukennedy/card/balance'.
Backend subscribes to those topics and publishes to 'rfid/kawacukennedy/card/topup' and 'rfid/kawacukennedy/card/pay'.
No wildcard subscriptions or cross-team topics.

functional.description: Safe Wallet Update: Balance change and transaction record are atomic.
acceptance_criteria: Database uses BEGIN IMMEDIATE and COMMIT for updates.
If any step fails, entire transaction is rolled back.
No partial updates or double-spending possible.

functional.description: HTTP API Endpoints: Backend exposes /topup and /pay.
acceptance_criteria: POST /topup accepts {uid, amount} and returns 202.
POST /pay accepts {uid, productId, quantity} and returns 202 or 402.
GET /products returns list of available products.

functional.description: Transaction Ledger: All top-ups and payments are stored in SQLite.
acceptance_criteria: Each transaction includes uid, type, amount, balance_after, timestamp.
Payment records include product_id and quantity.
Ledger is immutable and queryable.

functional.description: ESP8266 Edge Controller: Reads RFID and communicates via MQTT only.
acceptance_criteria: ESP8266 publishes card UID and current balance on card tap.
Subscribes to top-up and payment commands.
Updates local balance in EEPROM and publishes new balance after command.
No HTTP or WebSocket usage.

functional.description: Dashboard Separation: Admin and Cashier interfaces are clearly distinct.
acceptance_criteria: Two tabs or separate pages for Top-Up and Payment.
Each interface shows relevant fields and buttons.
Admin cannot access payment functions and vice versa.

functional.description: Error Handling: User-friendly error messages on the dashboard.
acceptance_criteria: Insufficient balance shows 'Declined: Insufficient funds'.
Invalid card shows 'Card not found'.
Network errors show 'Connection issue, retrying...'.

non_functional.description: Performance: HTTP responses under 200ms, WebSocket updates under 500ms.
acceptance_criteria: Load testing with 100 concurrent requests shows <200ms latency.
End-to-end payment cycle <1s.

non_functional.description: Reliability: MQTT connection auto-reconnects on failure.
acceptance_criteria: Backend and ESP8266 retry connection with exponential backoff.
No message loss during brief disconnections (QoS 1).

non_functional.description: Security: All HTTP traffic over HTTPS, MQTT over TLS.
acceptance_criteria: API endpoints use TLS 1.2+.
MQTT broker connection uses TLS.
Input validation on all endpoints.

non_functional.description: Usability: Dashboard follows iOS 26 design guidelines.
acceptance_criteria: Typography uses SF Pro, spacing consistent with 4px base.
Colors match system palette, contrast ratios met.
Motion and haptics (where applicable) follow spec.

non_functional.description: Maintainability: Codebase well-structured with TypeScript and documented.
acceptance_criteria: ESLint and Prettier configured.
README with setup and architecture.
Unit test coverage >80%.

user_stories.scenario: A student lost their card and got a new one.
user_stories.need: I need to top up the new card with the remaining balance from the old card.
user_stories.so_that: the student can continue using the system.
acceptance_tests: Admin enters new card UID and amount.
System confirms top-up and shows new balance.

user_stories.scenario: A customer wants to buy a coffee.
user_stories.need: I need to deduct the correct amount from the customer's card.
user_stories.so_that: the transaction is completed and balance updated.
acceptance_tests: Cashier selects product, quantity, taps card.
System shows approved and new balance.

user_stories.scenario: A new employee needs to be trained on the system.
user_stories.need: I need a clear interface that guides the top-up process.
acceptance_tests: Interface clearly labels fields and buttons.

user_stories.scenario: A customer tries to pay with insufficient balance.
user_stories.need: The system should reject the payment and explain why.
acceptance_tests: Payment attempt with low balance shows 'Declined: Insufficient funds'.

user_stories.scenario: I need to audit transactions for a specific card.
acceptance_tests: Database query returns all top-up and payment records for that UID.

user_stories.scenario: The network goes down during a top-up.
user_stories.need: The system should handle gracefully without corrupting data.
acceptance_tests: If MQTT publish fails, backend returns error and does not update DB.

user_stories.scenario: I tap my card to pay, but the dashboard doesn't update immediately.
user_stories.need: I want to see my new balance instantly.

user_stories.scenario: I need to understand the MQTT topic structure.
user_stories.need: Clear documentation of topics and payloads.
acceptance_tests: README includes topic list with examples.

user_stories.scenario: I accidentally enter a negative top-up amount.

edge_cases.handling: Backend and ESP8266 implement reconnection with exponential backoff. HTTP endpoints return 503 with 'Service unavailable' during broker outage. Pending commands are rejected.

On top-up, if card doesn't exist, backend creates a new card record with zero balance before applying top-up (or rejects if policy requires pre-registration). On payment, if card not found, return 404.

edge_cases.scenario: Balance mismatch between ESP8266 and database
edge_cases.handling: ESP8266 publishes its local balance. Backend compares with database; if mismatch, log and use database as source of truth (or initiate sync via command).

edge_cases.scenario: Concurrent top-up and payment on same card
edge_cases.handling: Database transactions with row locking prevent race conditions. Backend processes commands sequentially per card via MQTT (ESP8266 handles one command at a time).

edge_cases.scenario: Payment command sent but ESP8266 fails to update
edge_cases.handling: Backend sets a timeout (e.g., 5 seconds) for expected balance update. If not received, mark transaction as failed and no database change. Log error.
MQTT broker ACLs configured to allow only team-specific topics. Backend validates all incoming messages against expected patterns.
Use WAL mode and retry logic with backoff. If persistent, return 503.
Client auto-reconnects with exponential backoff. Missed updates are not replayed; dashboard can request current state via HTTP if needed.

balance.description: Current balance in smallest currency unit

components: WebSocket server attached to HTTP server
Broadcasts balance updates to all connected clients
Heartbeat mechanism to detect stale connections
Transaction helper functions for atomic updates

background_jobs.name: Transaction Log Pruning
background_jobs.description: Delete transactions older than 1 year to save space.
actions: DELETE FROM transactions WHERE created_at < datetime('now', '-1 year')

client_side.store: Zustand (or plain JS object with event listeners)

categories.handling: Log error, return 503, retry with backoff

recovery_strategies.circuit_breakers: For database failures, trip after 5 failures
recovery_strategies.fallbacks: Return cached balance if database unavailable for read-only operations

permissions.grant_mechanism: Simple role assignment (admin or cashier) via environment variable or basic auth

privacy_preserving.method: On-device aggregation with differential privacy (not implemented in MVP)

cryptography.storage: SQLite file encrypted at rest via OS-level (optional)

security.vulnerability_management: Regular dependency scanning with npm audit, Dependabot

compliance.gdpr: Data retention limited, user can request deletion

startup_optimization.lazy_loading: Database connection established on first request
startup_optimization.caching: Product list cached in memory for 5 minutes

scalability_strategies.horizontal_scaling: Multiple backend instances behind load balancer
scalability_strategies.sharding_strategies: By card UID range if using PostgreSQL
scalability_strategies.caching: Redis for balance cache to reduce database load
scalability_strategies.database_optimization: Switch from SQLite to PostgreSQL for high concurrency

integration_tests.pyramid: Test API with in-memory SQLite and mock MQTT broker

security_tests.dynamic: OWASP ZAP scan on deployed app

testing_plans.usability_tests: User testing with 5 participants to validate iOS design

user_docs: Admin guide: how to top up
Cashier guide: how to process payments

update_mechanism.overview: ESP8266 firmware can be updated OTA via Arduino IDE or platformio; backend updates via git pull and PM2 restart.
edge_cases: Update failure rolls back to previous version

security_enhancements: Input validation on all API endpoints

wizard_steps.title: Welcome
wizard_steps.content: This dashboard allows you to top up cards (Admin) or process payments (Cashier).

wizard_steps.title: Admin Interface
wizard_steps.content: Enter card UID and amount, then click TOP UP. The balance will update in real time.

wizard_steps.title: Cashier Interface
wizard_steps.content: Select product, quantity, and tap card. System will show approval and new balance.

onboarding.design_notes: Onboarding modal with large headline, SF Pro, and 'Continue' button using accent color.
